name: inline-loading
title: InlineLoading
description: A small inline status indicator showing an in-flight async operation and its outcome. The shadcn equivalent is just `Spinner` plus your own text and outcome icons — there is no state-machine component.

meta.docs: # InlineLoading A small inline status indicator showing an in-flight async operation and its outcome. The shadcn equivalent is just `Spinner` plus your own text and outcome icons — there is no state-machine component. ## When to use - **In-flight async indicator next to a control** — saving, uploading, validating. Render `` + a sibling `` while the operation is pending. - **Inside a button during submit** — ``. Drop the standalone wrapper entirely. - **Sized indicator in a dense surface** — pass `className="size-3"` (small) or `size-6` (large) on ``. Default is `size-4` (16px). - **Recoloured indicator** — `` inherits `currentColor`; set the parent's `text-…` class to recolour. ## When not to use - **Page-level loading state** — use a full-page or section `Skeleton`, not a single Spinner. - **Status that needs the previous-state context to read** ("Connecting…" / "Connected" / "Disconnected") — render the icon swap yourself; this primitive doesn't manage the state machine. - **Long-running progress with a known percentage** — use a `Progress` bar. ## Composition - shadcn ships only `Spinner` (a styled `lucide-react` `Loader2Icon` with `animate-spin` and `role="status"`). Status text, success/error icons, and state transitions are app-side composition. - Outcome icons: render `` for finished and `` for error — there is no built-in finished/error state. - For Carbon's `successDelay`: own the timeout in your component (`setTimeout(() => setStatus('idle'), 2000)`). - A11y: `` already carries `role="status"`. For success/error icons, add `{status}` or `aria-live="polite"` on the wrapper.
meta.migrationGuide:
# InlineLoading — Carbon → shadcn migration guide ## Scope mismatch Carbon's `InlineLoading` is a **stateful inline status indicator**: it shows a spinner with adjacent status text and transitions through `inactive` / `active` / `finished` / `error` states (each rendering a different icon and styling). It's typically used next to a "Save" button to show the in-flight save and its outcome. shadcn ships only the **`Spinner` primitive** — a styled `lucide-react` `Loader2Icon` with `animate-spin`. There is no built-in status text, no finished/error iconography, no state machine. Migrating means: - Replace the spinning indicator with ``. - Render status text yourself (just a sibling ``). - For `finished` and `error` states, render a different icon yourself (``, `` from `lucide-react` are the canonical choices). - Manage the state transitions in your component (typically already lives in your form / mutation logic). ## Mapping | Carbon `status` | shadcn equivalent | |---|---| | `inactive` | render nothing (or ``) | | `active` | `` (spinning Loader2 icon) | | `finished` | `` | | `error` | `` | | Concept | Carbon | shadcn | |---|---|---| | Wrapper | `` | `
{text}
` | | Status icon | implicit (chosen by `status`) | render manually per state | | Status text | `description` prop | sibling `` | | Success message | `successDelay` (auto-fade after success) | manage with `setTimeout` in your component | | Spinner | always present | render only when active | ## Carbon features missing in shadcn - **`status` prop with state-driven iconography** — Carbon flips between spinner, checkmark, X based on `status`. shadcn requires you to render the right icon yourself based on your loading/success/error state. - **`description` text slot** — Carbon's per-state text. shadcn: render a sibling `` with state-specific text. - **`successDelay`** — Carbon auto-hides the finished state after N ms. shadcn: use `setTimeout(() => setStatus('inactive'), 2000)` in your component. - **Animation transitions between states** — Carbon cross-fades between icons. shadcn: no built-in; if you want it, add Tailwind animation classes or use Framer Motion. - **`iconDescription`** (a11y) — Carbon's SR description for the status icon. shadcn: `` ships `aria-label="Loading"` already; for finished/error icons, add `…`. - **`className` on the wrapper that affects layout** — Carbon's wrapper is a flex container. shadcn: you own the wrapper; style with Tailwind as needed. - **Skeleton variant (``)** — Carbon ships skeletons. shadcn has only `Spinner` + the generic `skeleton` primitive. ## shadcn features missing in Carbon - **Standalone Spinner usable anywhere** — `` works inside buttons, badges, tiles, cards. Carbon's spinner is bundled with the inline-loading layout. - **Tailwind composability** — `` for small, `size-6` for large, `text-primary` to recolour. Carbon's spinner has fixed sizing. - **`role="status"`** — shadcn `Spinner` carries `role="status"` so screen readers announce "Loading" automatically. Carbon's status text is announced too, but the icon-only fallback is shadcn's better default. ## Behavioural differences - **Default size** — Carbon: 16px spinner with surrounding label spacing. shadcn `Spinner`: `size-4` (16px), no surrounding spacing. - **Animation** — Carbon: custom CSS spinner animation (matches Carbon design language). shadcn: `animate-spin` Tailwind class on Lucide's `Loader2Icon` (~360° rotation per second). - **Colour** — Carbon: blue (`--cds-interactive-01`) by default. shadcn: inherits `currentColor` (whatever text colour the parent has). - **Accessibility** — Both announce "loading" / status text to SRs. ## Migration checklist 1. Decide whether you need full state-machine behaviour (`active`/`finished`/`error`) or just a spinner. Most usages of `InlineLoading` in Camunda webapps are "show spinner during async action then disappear" — that's just `` conditionally rendered. 2. Replace `` with: ```tsx
{text}
``` 3. For `status="finished"`: render `` + `` (not a ``). 4. For `status="error"`: render `` + ``. 5. For `successDelay`: own the timeout in your component (`useEffect(() => { const t = setTimeout(() => setStatus('inactive'), 2000); return () => clearTimeout(t); }, [status])`). 6. If used inside a button (the most common Carbon pattern): replace with `` — drop the standalone InlineLoading entirely. 7. For a11y on success/error icons: add `{status}` next to the icon (or use `aria-live="polite"` on the wrapper div).
